target.title: Bug: openclaw-weixin startAccount ???,?????????? session
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix manifest-only/read-only channel account resolution so route-safe account IDs can resolve config entries whose raw provider IDs contain opaque characters like `@` or `.`.
- Reuse the shared normalized account lookup path and keep blocked object keys out of normalized fallback matches.
- Add regression coverage for both the openclaw-weixin-style raw account key and the blocked-key default-account case.

## Linked context

Addresses #93556.

- Fix classification: root-cause bug fix, scoped to external channel/plugin account config resolution.

- Maintainer-ready confidence: high. The fix is covered by focused routing and read-only plugin contract tests, plus live openclaw-weixin startup proof showing the external channel account starts and reaches monitor state.

- Root cause: manifest channel account listing normalized raw account keys for routing, but manifest channel account resolution used a different mapping contract by looking up the already-normalized route ID directly in the raw `accounts` object. A config key such as `59000514e8ad@im.bot` was listed as `59000514e8ad-im-bot`, but the resolver could not map that normalized ID back to the original raw config entry.

- Why this is root-cause fix: the resolver now uses the same normalized account ID contract as the lister, so raw provider IDs and route-safe IDs map through one resolver invariant. The shared normalized lookup also filters blocked raw keys during fallback matching, preserving the existing prototype-pollution guard at the lookup source instead of adding a downstream fallback.

- What did NOT change: no plugin manifest format, gateway startup behavior, account ID normalization rules, generated metadata, provider-specific openclaw-weixin code, or unrelated channel routing behavior changed.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: a live external `openclaw-weixin` channel account whose raw provider ID normalizes to a route-safe account ID can be loaded by the Gateway, started through the channel plugin, and reported as running instead of being dropped before `startAccount`.

- Real environment tested: macOS Darwin 25.5.0, Node v24.15.0, OpenClaw 2026.6.2, worktree SHA `3b8e699`, external `@tencent-weixin/openclaw-weixin@2.4.3`, isolated OpenClaw home, live QR login with Weixin.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```bash
  pnpm openclaw plugins install "@tencent-weixin/openclaw-weixin@2.4.3" --force
  pnpm openclaw config set plugins.entries.openclaw-weixin.enabled true
  pnpm openclaw channels login --channel openclaw-weixin
  pnpm openclaw config set gateway.mode local
  pnpm openclaw config set gateway.port 18790
  pnpm dev gateway --port 18790
  pnpm openclaw channels status --probe
  pnpm test src/channels/plugins/read-only.test.ts src/routing/account-lookup.test.ts
  ```

- Evidence after fix: copied and sanitized terminal output from the live OpenClaw Gateway and channel probe.

  ```text
  Installed plugin: openclaw-weixin
  Restart the gateway to load plugins.

  已将此 OpenClaw 连接到微信。
  ```

  ```text
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway --port 18790
2026-06-17T01:02:11.349+08:00 [gateway] auto-enabled plugins for this runtime without writing config:
- openclaw-weixin configured, enabled automatically.
2026-06-17T01:02:11.617+08:00 [plugins] loading openclaw-weixin from <proof-home>/.openclaw/npm/projects/tencent-weixin-openclaw-weixin-7783ac86ba/node_modules/@tencent-weixin/openclaw-weixin/dist/index.js
2026-06-17T01:02:11.730+08:00 [gateway] http server listening (10 plugins: acpx, bonjour, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, openclaw-weixin, phone-control, talk-voice; 0.5s)
2026-06-17T01:02:11.731+08:00 [gateway] starting channels and sidecars...
2026-06-17T01:02:11.766+08:00 [openclaw-weixin] [<route-safe-weixin-account-id>] starting weixin provider (https://ilinkai.weixin.qq.com)
2026-06-17T01:02:11.779+08:00 [gateway] ready
2026-06-17T01:02:12.021+08:00 [openclaw-weixin] weixin monitor started (https://ilinkai.weixin.qq.com, account=<route-safe-weixin-account-id>)
2026-06-17T01:02:12.021+08:00 [openclaw-weixin] [weixin] no previous sync buf, starting fresh
2026-06-17T01:02:21.833+08:00 [plugins] loading openclaw-weixin from <proof-home>/.openclaw/npm/projects/tencent-weixin-openclaw-weixin-7783ac86ba/node_modules/@tencent-weixin/openclaw-weixin/dist/index.js
  ```

  ```text
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs channels status --probe
Checking channel status (probe)…
Gateway reachable.
- openclaw-weixin <route-safe-weixin-account-id>: enabled, configured, running

Tip: https://docs.openclaw.ai/cli#status adds gateway health probes to status output (requires a reachable gateway).
  ```

- Observed result after fix: the live external `openclaw-weixin` plugin loaded from its installed npm package, the Gateway started channels and sidecars, the route-safe Weixin account ID entered `starting weixin provider`, the Weixin monitor started against `https://ilinkai.weixin.qq.com`, and `channels status --probe` reported that same account as `enabled, configured, running`.

- What was not tested: no inbound Weixin DM was sent through to an agent reply in this proof; this live check covers QR auth, account resolution, channel startup, provider start, monitor start, and Gateway status for the fixed account path. The exact issue-reported `@tencent-weixin/openclaw-weixin@2.1.8` package was not used for live startup because the current OpenClaw installer rejects that npm package for missing compiled runtime output; the live startup proof used the current official 2.x package line.

## Tests and validation

- Plugin test / extension-specific contract coverage: `src/channels/plugins/read-only.test.ts`
- Routing helper regression coverage: `src/routing/account-lookup.test.ts`
- Validation result: `2 passed (2)` test files, `36 passed (36)` tests.

  ```text
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/channels/plugins/read-only.test.ts src/routing/account-lookup.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 <repo>


 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  6 passed (6)
   Start at  01:05:14
   Duration  116ms (transform 25ms, setup 0ms, import 34ms, tests 2ms, environment 0ms)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.channels.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 <repo>


 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  30 passed (30)
   Start at  01:05:16
   Duration  4.97s (transform 1.44s, setup 191ms, import 1.74s, tests 2.95s, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 9.54s
  ```

## Risk checklist

- [x] The diff is scoped to manifest channel account resolution and normalized account lookup.
- [x] Exact account keys still take precedence before normalized fallback matching.
- [x] Blocked object keys remain excluded from normalized fallback matching.
- [x] No dependency, schema, migration, generated metadata, or public plugin API changes.
- [x] No local paths, credentials, or private logs are included in this PR body.

## Current review state

Ready for maintainer review after focused regression tests and live openclaw-weixin startup proof on the current head.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
